questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The following sentence has been split into segments. One of them may contain an error. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer.No one were / present when I / entered the marriage hall.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “No one were”.“No one” is a singular pronoun and requires a singular verb form. In this case the correct verb form is “was” instead of “were”. The correct sentence should be “No one was present when I entered the hall”.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.To walk or move at a slow, relaxed pace.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “amble”.Amble: to walk or move at a slow, relaxed pace. Slither: move smoothly over a surface with a twisting or oscillating motion. Crawl: move forward on the hands and knees or by dragging the body close to the ground. Sprint: run at full speed over a short distance.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer.It was / the very well-directed film / and we enjoyed it.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “the very well-directed film”.In this sentence article “a” should be use instead of “the” article. The correct sentence “It was a very well-directed film and we enjoyed it.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.showy
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “ostentatious”.Ostentatious: attracting or seeking to attract attention, admiration or envy often by gaudiness or obviousness.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.Reciprocate
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Retaliate”.Reciprocate: act, feel, or give mutually or in return. Retaliate: make an attack in return for a similar attack. Forgive: to grant pardon for or remission of (an offense, debt, etc.). Excuse: A reason or explanation given to justify a fault or offence. Rotate: move or cause to move in a circle round an axis or Centre.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice.They surrendered all weapons.
options: All weapons was surrendered by them
All weapons were surrendered by them.
All weapons is surrendered by they.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “All weapons were surrendered by them”.In simple past: Active voice Passive voice Subject + V2 + object object + was/ were + V3 + by + subject. They surrendered all weapons. All weapons were surrendered by them.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain a grammatical error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer.If Parinita fails / this year it reflect badly / on her career.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “this year it reflect badly”.If and since are subordinate conjunctions. Subordinate conjunctions join two clauses. When sentence are divided into clauses, one will be the principal clause and the other will be subordinate clause. Here subordinate clause depends on the principal clause. Example: If you eat a lot, you will gain weight. So the correct sentence: If Parinita fails this year, it will reflect badly on her career.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Conceited
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Modest”.Conceited: excessively proud of oneself; Vain. Glum: sad and quiet. Unhappy: sad. Modest: not talking too much about your own abilities, good qualities, etc.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Mitigate
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Exaggerate”.Mitigate: make (something bad) less severe, serious, or painful. Dull: lacking interest and excitement. Exaggerate: represent (something) as being larger, better, or worse than it really is. Alleviate: make (suffering, deficiency or a problem) less severe. Ease: absence of difficulty or effort.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate option to substitute the bold segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No substitution required.’An Asiatic lioness has given birth to a cub in the Gir national park.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “no substitution required”.In the given subject “An Asiatic lioness” is a singular subject. Hence, a singular auxiliary verb ‘has’ is used. ‘Has’ is followed by the ‘past participle’ and ‘given’ is the past participle of the verb ‘Give’. Thus, the correct answer is option A.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the BOLD segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No substitution required’.The coach is assured the players that they will look into the matter.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “has assured”.There is grammatical error in the given sentence. “has assured” should be used instead of “is assured”. The given sentence is in present perfect tense. So, the rule is: Subject + has/have + V3 + object. Thus, correct sentence is “The coach has assured the players that they will look into the matter.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.Haul over the coals
options: Find it very difficult to buy something
To scold someone severely for an error
Purchase something in very less amount
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “To scold someone severely for an error”.Haul over the coals: To scold, reprimand, or reprove someone severely for an error of mistake.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.Lurid
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Shocking”.Lurid: Presented in vividly, shocking or sensational terms. Ecstasy: an overwhelming feeling of great happiness or joyful excitement. Abundant: existing or available in large quantities; plentiful. Shocking: Causing indignation or disgust; offensive. Prevent: keep (something) from happening.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.One of the / curious children / want to / rip open a toy.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “want to”.If ‘of’ is used after each, every, one etc., the noun or pronoun that comes immediately after ‘of’ will be plural in form, and the verb comes in the latter part of the sentence will be singular in form. The correct sentence: one of the curious children wants to rip open a toy.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.Study of the forms and features of land surfaces
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “topography”.Topography: Study of the forms and features of land surfaces. Seismology: the study of earthquakes Geography: the study of the diverse environments, places, and spaces of Earth’s surface and their interactions. Geology: study of earth.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.Sunita is unable to continue working in this office. Hard work is ________ on her health.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “taking a toll”.Taking a toll: have an adverse effect. Keeping an eye: to watch closely or carefully. Blowing hot and cold: keep changing mind about someone or something. Meet her both ends: to have just enough money to pay for the things that you need. Hence, the most appropriate answer is “taking a toll”.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.Radhika says the treatment is safe, and then in the same breath says that patients should be warned about possible side-effects.
options: Say two contradictory things at the same time
Able to get a foul smell
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “say two contradictory things at the same time”.In the same breath: indicate that someone says two very different or contradictory things.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice.Kanak handles all tasks efficiently.
options: All tasks were handled efficiently by Kanak.
All tasks are being handled efficiently by Kanak.
All tasks have been handled efficiently by Kanak.
All tasks are handled efficiently by Kanak.
questions.explanation: the correct answer is “All tasks are handled efficiently by Kanak”.Simple present tense: Active voice passive voice Subject + verb (s/es) + object object + is/am/are + V3 + subject The correct passive voice: All tasks are handled efficiently by Kanak.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech.Pushpa asked me when I had booked the movie tickets.
options: Pushpa said to me, “When are you booking the movie tickets?”
Pushpa said to me, “When did you booked the movie tickets?”
Pushpa said to me, “When did you book the movie tickets?”
Pushpa said to me, “When do you book the movie tickets?”
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Pushpa said to me, “When did you book the movie tickets?”The given sentence is in indirect speech we have to change it into direct speech. ‘Asked’ will be changed into ‘said to’. Commas and inverted commas will be added. ‘I’ will be changed into ‘you’ because in direct to indirect transformation second-person pronouns are changed according to the object of the reporting verb. Thus, the correct answer is “Pushpa said to me, “When did you book the movie tickets?”.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “omelette”.Omelette: a dish made by mixing eggs together and frying them. Circuit: the complete path of an electric current. Genuine: truly what something is said to be; authentic. Manners: a way in which a thing is done or happens.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.During the pandemic, Viren, a regular platelet donor, was stuck outside Mumbai for 14 weeks. A constant thought was the missed donation dates. He (1) ______ there would be a need because, (2) ______ the lockdown restrictions, people on chemotherapy were being treated and (3) ______ keep needing platelets. When he returned home, he discovered that the situation was grim as many regular donors (4) ______ coming either out of fear or because travel was difficult. So, he formed a small (5) ______ of donors, arranged for transport and letters from TMC Hospital to validate our travel and they started donating in July 2020. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no.1.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “knew”.The given passage is in past tense. Hence, the second form of verb “knew” is come in blank1.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.During the pandemic, Viren, a regular platelet donor, was stuck outside Mumbai for 14 weeks. A constant thought was the missed donation dates. He (1)______ there would be a need because, (2)______ the lockdown restrictions, people on chemotherapy were being treated and (3)______ keep needing platelets. When he returned home, he discovered that the situation was grim as many regular donors (4)______ coming either out of fear or because travel was difficult. So, he formed a small (5)______ of donors, arranged for transport and letters from TMC Hospital to validate our travel and they started donating in July 2020. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no.2.
questions.explanation: the correct answer is “despite”.Without: in the absence of. Unlike: different from; not similar to. Despite: without being affected by; in spite of. Expect for: regard (something) as likely to happen.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.During the pandemic, Viren, a regular platelet donor, was stuck outside Mumbai for 14 weeks. A constant thought was the missed donation dates. He (1)______ there would be a need because, (2)______ the lockdown restrictions, people on chemotherapy were being treated and (3)______ keep needing platelets. When he returned home, he discovered that the situation was grim as many regular donors (4)______ coming either out of fear or because travel was difficult. So, he formed a small (5)______ of donors, arranged for transport and letters from TMC Hospital to validate our travel and they started donating in July 2020. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no.3.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “would”.The first clause of the sentence is in the past tense which is connected by the conjunction “and”. The clause after the conjunction will also be in the past tense. The verb “would” indicates habitual past action. Hence, the correct answer is “would”.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.During the pandemic, Viren, a regular platelet donor, was stuck outside Mumbai for 14 weeks. A constant thought was the missed donation dates. He (1)______ there would be a need because, (2)______ the lockdown restrictions, people on chemotherapy were being treated and (3)______ keep needing platelets. When he returned home, he discovered that the situation was grim as many regular donors (4)______ coming either out of fear or because travel was difficult. So, he formed a small (5)______ of donors, arranged for transport and letters from TMC Hospital to validate our travel and they started donating in July 2020. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no.4.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “had stopped”.The given sentence is an example of the past perfect tense. Structure of a past perfect tense is “had + past participle form (V3). We need to use the verb in the past participle form “stopped” along with the helping verb “had”.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.During the pandemic, Viren, a regular platelet donor, was stuck outside Mumbai for 14 weeks. A constant thought was the missed donation dates. He (1)______ there would be a need because, (2)______ the lockdown restrictions, people on chemotherapy were being treated and (3)______ keep needing platelets. When he returned home, he discovered that the situation was grim as many regular donors (4)______ coming either out of fear or because travel was difficult. So, he formed a small (5)______ of donors, arranged for transport and letters from TMC Hospital to validate our travel and they started donating in July 2020. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no.5.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “group”.Let’s look at the meaning of given words. Group: a number of people or things that are located, gathered, or classed together. Corporation: a large company or group of companies authorized to act as a single entity and recognized as such in law. Party: A social gathering of invited guests, typically involving eating, drinking, and entertainment. Gang: An organized group of criminals.
